articleTitle: Oral and topical peptides for skin aging: systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
takeaways: Based on the systematic review of randomized controlled trials, both oral and topical peptide therapies demonstrated measurable improvements in skin hydration, elasticity, wrinkle reduction, and brightness compared to placebo treatments
Peptide-based interventions work by enhancing collagen synthesis and strengthening extracellular matrix integrity, addressing both intrinsic aging processes and environmental damage to skin structure

The meta-analysis found that peptide treatments effectively target multiple aging markers simultaneously - including wrinkles, reduced elasticity, and skin roughness - suggesting a comprehensive approach to skin aging management
Both delivery methods (oral supplementation and topical application) showed efficacy in clinical trials, indicating peptides can work through different routes to improve skin aging parameters
